A message signal m(t) = Amtanh(βt) is applied to a delta modulator, where ‘Am’ and ‘β’ are real positive constants. The minimum step-size required by the delta modulator to eliminate the slopeoverload distortion for the given message signal is, [Ts: Sampling interval]

Correct answer: A
Solution

(a)

Q3 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

In a (6, 3) block code, the parity equations are p1 = d2 ⊕ d3, p2 = d1 ⊕ d3, p3 = d1 ⊕ d2
The minimum hamming distance is,

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

Correct answer: C
Solution

Q5 Not checked

Not attempted

Let denote the probabilities of symbol error for the in-phase and quadrature channels of a narrow-band digital communication system. The average probability of symbol error for the overall system is given by,

Correct answer: C
Solution

(c)

 
 
Q6 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

Consider voice transmission using BPSK signalling at a transmission rate of 4.8 kbps and a bandwidth efficiency of 1 bps/Hz. In order to increase the data rate to 38.4 kbps with the symbol rate to remain at 4800 symbols/sec, the number of points required in the signal constellation will be _______.

NAT
Correct answer: 256
Solution

256 (256 - 256)

 
 
Q7 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

The input signal s(t) to the matched filter is given by,

 
 
If the impulse response of the matched filter is h(t), the value of the integral  is ____________,
NAT
Correct answer: -3
Solution

-3 (-3 - -3)

 
 
Q8 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +1 / -0
Not attempted

A 5 kHz bandpass channel is to be used for M-ary PSK transmission of data at a rate of 10500 bits/sec.
If we use a signal pulse with a raised cosine spectrum having an excess bandwidth of atleast 70%, the minimum value of M is _______.

NAT
Correct answer: 16
Solution

16 (16 - 16)

Solution

Q10 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

An FSK system transmits binary data at the rate of 2.5 × 106 bits per second. During the course of transmission, AWGN of zero mean and power spectral density 5 × 10–20 W/Hz is added to the signal. In the absence of noise, the amplitude of the received sinusoidal wave for digit 1 or 0 is 1 μV.
The average probability of symbol error, assuming coherent detection is,

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

 
 
Q11 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0.66
Not attempted

In a digital communication receiver, the input to the threshold comparator is either 5 V or –5 V for ‘1’ and ‘0’ transmission respectively. The received signal is affected by AWGN with variance 0.5 V2. If p(0) =  the threshold voltage of the comparator is,

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

Solution

Q13 Not checked

Not attempted

For the channel and message probabilities given in the below figure, the decisions about the transmitted message for each possible received response is made using MAP decoding technique.

 
 
Choose the correct decision according to MAP decoding technique:
Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)

 
 
Q14 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

A delta modulation system samples a sinusoidal sample of audio data at 256 kHz. Consider the bandwidth of the signal to be 16 kHz and a peak amplitude of 4 V. The Granular noise power generated at the output of DM receiver in order to prevent slope overload is _________ mW.
(Rounded off to one decimal place)

NAT
Correct answer: 51.4
Solution

51.4 (51.0 - 51.9)

 
Q15 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Consider a threshold detector with input R = X + N, where X is the signal level at the input of the detector due to transmitted binary data and N is a noise variable which is characterised by the probability density function fN(n) = 0.5e–|n|.
X takes the values : +1 with probability of 1/3
–1 with probability of 2/3
If the threshold value of the detector is decided in an optimum way using maximum a posteriori
(MAP) criteria, the value of optimum threshold voltage is _________. [Rounded off to two decimal places]

NAT
Correct answer: 0.35
Solution

0.35 (0.30 - 0.40)

 
 
Q16 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

The amplitude of a random signal is uniformly distributed between –2 V and 2 V.
If the positive values of the signal are uniformly quantized with a step size of 0.07 V, and the negative values are uniformly quantized with a step size of 0.5 V, the signal to quantization noise ratio of the given PCM system is __________. (rounded off to two decimal places)

NAT
Correct answer: 125.54
Solution

125.54 (125.00 - 126.00)

 
Q17 Not checked
Technical • ELECTRONICS ENGINEERING • +2 / -0
Not attempted

Signals S1(t) and S2(t) shown in below figure (a) are used as orthonormal basis in a binary data transmission system.

 
 
The vector representations  of the two signal waveforms used for transmission are shown in below figure (b).
 
The probability of error if transmission is over an AWGN channel with noise having power spectral density equal to 0.5 W/Hz is Q(c).
Select the correct values of a, b, c from the options given below:
Correct answer: N/A
Solution

(b, c)
